## What Changed

- Added an installed MCP entrypoint: `touch-browser mcp`
- Standalone bundles now ship the local MCP bridge/runtime so desktop MCP hosts can invoke the installed command directly
- Updated the checked-in MCP examples and user docs to use the installed command flow first, while keeping the repo-local Node launcher as a compatibility path
- Added automated coverage for the installed MCP path in both the MCP smoke gate and the standalone install/update/uninstall lifecycle gate

## User Impact

- Personal MCP setup can now use:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "touch-browser": {
      "command": "touch-browser",
      "args": ["mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

- A repo checkout is no longer required for the primary local MCP setup path
- `touch-browser update` continues to update the installed binary; MCP hosts pick up the new version on the next process restart

## Notes

- This release productizes the local stdio MCP path only
- Remote HTTP MCP hosting is not part of this release
